blurb: The Beatitudes reveal the Stranger God's radical inversion of the Demiurge's value system—where law rewards the righteous and punishes the poor, the Father declares the destitute and mourning to be blessed. These are not promises of future compensation for present suffering, but proclamations of present reality: those whom the world's systems have crushed are precisely those most ready to receive the kingdom that operates by grace rather than merit. The Father's benedictions fall upon empty hands and broken hearts because these alone have room for the gift that cannot be earned.

verses.text: And he came down among them, and stood in the plain, and the company of his disciples, and a great multitude of people out of all Judaea and Jerusalem, and from the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon, which came to hear him, and to be healed of their diseases;
And they that were vexed with unclean spirits: and they were healed.
And the whole multitude sought to touch him: for there went virtue out of him, and healed them all.
And he lifted up his eyes on his disciples, and said, Blessed be ye poor: for yours is the kingdom of God.
